operating_state: Worldwide sales increased 4.3% to $88.8 billion driven by 5.9% volume growth offset by 1.6% negative currency impact. Innovative Medicine grew with Oncology surging driven by DARZALEX and ERLEADA gains, while Immunology declined from STELARA biosimilar competition beginning July 2024 in Europe. MedTech increased with Cardiovascular delivering strong growth from Shockwave acquisition and Electrophysiology expansion, while Surgery declined from China volume-based procurement pressures.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved as percentage of sales driven by favorable oncology patient mix and elimination of COVID-19 vaccine exit costs, partially offset by Shockwave inventory step-up charges. Research and development expenses rose to 19.4% of sales from 17.7% driven by Yellow Jersey acquisition for NM26 bispecific antibody rights and expanded clinical programs. MedTech segment margin compressed due to acquisition integration costs and increased intangible amortization.
material_events: Completed Shockwave Medical acquisition for $13.1 billion in May 2024 funded through senior unsecured notes issuance. Acquired Yellow Jersey for $1.25 billion securing NM26 bispecific antibody rights and V-Wave expanding heart failure portfolio. Divested remaining Kenvue stake through debt-for-equity exchange resulting in $0.4 billion loss. Recorded $5.0 billion incremental talc litigation charge bringing total reserve to $12.0 billion as Red River filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy pursuing comprehensive settlement.
macro_exposures: International operations representing 43% of sales face currency headwinds with 1.6% negative translation impact and geopolitical risks. China volume-based procurement pressures multiple MedTech franchises while STELARA biosimilar competition intensified in Europe with U.S. entry anticipated January 2025. Multiple countries designated highly inflationary requiring specialized accounting treatment. Pillar Two minimum tax legislation expected to increase effective tax rate by 1.0% in fiscal 2025.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued STELARA biosimilar pressure with U.S. market entry January 2025 while COVID-19 vaccine revenue effectively eliminated. Orthopaedics restructuring program completion expected by end fiscal 2025. Company maintains investment grade ratings supporting commercial paper program plus new credit facility. Inflation Reduction Act subjects certain products to government pricing beginning 2026 with potential expansion.
uncertainty_profile: Talc litigation remains unresolved with Red River bankruptcy pursuing settlement over 25 years covering 99.75% of pending lawsuits, facing venue transfer motions and dismissal challenges. Patent challenges threaten key pharmaceutical exclusivity periods with ongoing litigation against generic manufacturers. Janssen's constitutional challenge to IRA Medicare Drug Price Negotiation Program remains under appeal with approximately 3,500 opioid-related lawsuits continuing.
